digest.component_or_area: store-level compatibility record and fail-closed capability gate
propositions: A committed store-level compatibility record (format_version + required_capabilities) is written atomically at a fixed path and read by a gate that fails closed on unknown version or unrecognized required capability.

The gate is inserted at the single structural choke point inside lock.acquire() so all lock-based mutations are covered without enumerating call-sites, plus explicit gates in fsck_recover_cli and reconcile._apply_mutations() for the two un-locked mutation paths.

An absent record is treated as legacy version 0 pass-through (not blocked) and is opportunistically written by an idempotent ensure unit, so a missed or fail-silent run_ensures sweep never blocks writes.
A corrupt or unreadable record fails closed rather than being treated as absent, with a diagnostic naming the parse error and record path.
run_ensures must re-raise StoreIncompatibleError before its broad except Exception so the fail-closed gate is not swallowed on the ensure-sweep path for init, MCP-boot, and fsck --repair.

Read-only commands (fsck diagnostic, show, list, search) stay operable under an incompatible record and surface a machine-checkable compat_error object, while fsck repair mode is gated; a pre-v1.0 binary ignores the new file for rollback safety.
